titleStmt.title: Letter from William Upton Tyrrell to William J.H. Tyrrell 26, March, 1920
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: William Upton Tyrrell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Expresses love for his father and gratitude for for an engagement present.

msIdentifier.repository: OPW-Maynooth University Archive & Research Centre at Castletown

correspAction.placeName: 1st Bn Royal Irish Rifles, Albany Barracks, Parkhurst, Isle Of Wight, England
Ballindoolin House, Ballindoolin, Edenderry, Kings Co., Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
197 B ROYAL IRISH RIFLES ALBANY BARRACKS PARKHURST 1 OF W 26/3/20. My dearest Daddy, Your letter of 24th with your most
generous cheque came tonight - Really you
are the very best and dearest that was lived
and I simplty can't thank you enough. You
have always always been to me the very
very best Father that any boy could
possibly have. and you have always been
far far too good and generous. Everyone
of you have always been doing things
and giving me things and I'll never
never be able to repay the half of your
kindness. All I can hope is that I will
always be a credit to you all. Even
when I'm married my home will always
be with you and we shall sepnd the
greratest part of our leave with you. And
we shall always look frward to t our
leave too. And I specially look forward to
the time when we shall have a little house
of our own and you can all come and
stay with us. And I know Nellie is looking
forward to that too. She is always going
to consider you a 2nd Father. And we are
both going to be the very happiest two that
have ever lived. I do hope that you will
be able to come over for the 10th. I do so
want you to be there. I'm going to buy
something with that present that will really
last me forever & ever. I haven't quite decided
yet what it will be but it will be
something very very nice. John's present arrived tonight and I'm
writing to him. Also to Mrs Williams of Grange. I'm back in Barracks again. Very best love to all Your most afficionate sone Willie
